steps.title: Identify the pronoun's role
steps.content: The sentence says barnacles use secretions to "attach ______ to a sea turtle shell." The blank is the object of "attach" — we need a pronoun that refers back to the subject "turtle barnacles." Because the barnacles are attaching themselves (subject = object), a reflexive pronoun is required. Because "turtle barnacles" is plural, the correct reflexive is "themselves."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'it': Singular, non-reflexive — doesn't match the plural antecedent "turtle barnacles." ✗
  • Choice B — 'themselves': Plural reflexive — correctly refers back to "turtle barnacles" and signals the barnacles act on themselves. ✓
  • Choice C — 'them': Non-reflexive plural — creates an unclear reference; it's ambiguous what "them" refers to. ✗
  • Choice D — 'itself': Singular reflexive — doesn't agree in number with the plural "turtle barnacles." ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
